title: [TASK] Set up PostgreSQL in Docker with automated migration/change script mechanism

conversation_log.summary: Two acceptance criteria require revision before the task is testable. (1) Criterion 2 states the Compose file is at the monorepo root, which directly contradicts the Architect's approved correction that it lives at docker/docker-compose.yml per ADR-004. A test cannot simultaneously satisfy the criterion as written and the approved implementation guidance. (2) Criterion 11 contains an untestable policy assertion: 'no schema changes are applied outside of the migration mechanism' cannot be verified by any test — there is no runtime artifact proving the absence of all possible external schema changes. A rewrite to specify inspectable file locations was suggested. Criteria 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, and 12 are testable as written.

Revised two acceptance criteria per Tester rejection feedback. (1) Criterion 2: replaced 'at the monorepo root' with 'docker/docker-compose.yml (the project's canonical Compose file per ADR-004)' — aligning with the Architect's approved correction and ADR-004. (2) Criterion 11: replaced the untestable policy assertion ('no schema changes are applied outside of the migration mechanism') with a statically-verifiable statement specifying exact inspectable locations: docker/docker-compose.yml init scripts, Dockerfile commands, application startup code, and any file outside packages/database/migrations/ must not contain ad-hoc schema changes. Also updated 'Files Likely to Change' to reference docker/docker-compose.yml instead of the repo-root docker-compose.yml, consistent with criterion 2 correction. Restarting Architect + Tester sign-off process.

Revised acceptance criteria are architecturally consistent. Criterion 2 now correctly references docker/docker-compose.yml per ADR-004. Criterion 11 is now statically verifiable. All 12 criteria align with architecture.md (§6 Environment Configuration, §External Integrations > PostgreSQL, ADR-004 Canonical Folder Structure). Standing constraints: packages/database/ contains only migration scripts and schema artifacts (package name @cv-tool/database); prefer plain-SQL migration tool; use existing DATABASE_URL or POSTGRES_* variables; extend existing db service in docker/docker-compose.yml.

All 12 revised acceptance criteria are testable as written. Criteria 2 and 11 were previously rejected and have been corrected: criterion 2 now correctly references docker/docker-compose.yml (statically verifiable by file path and content); criterion 11 is now a static-inspection constraint against named files and directories rather than an untestable policy assertion. Test methods confirmed for all criteria: criteria 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12 are verifiable via static inspection of repository files; criterion 9 is verifiable via runtime integration test (docker compose up + SQL query). No criterion is vague, subjective, or dependent on undelivered future tasks.

Implemented PostgreSQL migration setup using dbmate. Added packages/database/@cv-tool/database workspace with migration 20260304000000_create_test_entries.sql (creates test_entries table with id, label, created_at columns; seeds one row). Added migrate service to docker/docker-compose.yml using ghcr.io/amacneil/dbmate:2 that runs automatically on stack start, depends on db healthcheck, and backend depends on migrate completing successfully. Added POSTGRES_PORT to .env.example. Added ADR-005 to docs/tech-decisions.md documenting dbmate choice. PR opened on branch task/11-db-migrations-setup.

decisions.approach: Use dbmate (ghcr.io/amacneil/dbmate:2) as a standalone Docker-based migration tool. Migrations are plain SQL files under packages/database/migrations/ using the -- migrate:up / -- migrate:down convention. A dedicated 'migrate' service in docker-compose.yml runs dbmate automatically on stack start, ensuring zero manual intervention. The backend service depends on migrate completing successfully before starting.
risks: The migrate service uses 'restart: on-failure' to handle transient startup races. If the db service is slow to become healthy, dbmate may exit with a non-zero code and retry — this is correct behaviour but may cause a brief delay before the backend starts.

The packages/database/ workspace is included via the 'packages/*' glob in root package.json workspaces — no explicit entry is needed, but this depends on the glob remaining broad. If the workspaces array is ever changed to explicit entries, packages/database/ must be added manually.

dbmate's --no-dump-schema flag suppresses schema.sql generation. If a schema.sql snapshot is ever needed for tooling or review, this flag would need to be removed and the generated file added to .gitignore or tracked.
